When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state:
# Top row: b(blue), N(green), Y(yellow), p(purple) - order is: blue, green, yellow, purple
# Middle frame slots: [green, gray, gray, gray] - need to fill with blue, yellow, purple in that order
# Bottom remaining: p(purple) at (58,27), b(blue) at (58,35), Y(yellow) at (58,43)
# The top order is: b, N, Y, p = blue, green, yellow, purple
# So I need to fill slots 2,3,4 with: blue, yellow, purple

# Let me click bottom blue block at (58,35) then slot 2 at (29,28)
m1 = {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 35}
m2 = {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 28}
action([m1, m2])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"world_model": "Level 1. Top: 4 colored blocks (b, N, Y, p) - 20px each at row 3. Middle: red frame with 4 slots. Bottom: 4 colored blocks (N, p, b, Y) - 16px each at row 58.
goal_model: Fill the 4 slots in the red frame by matching the top colors order of top colors.
action_model: Click bottom colored block to select it, then click a gray dot slot to place it in a gray dot slot.
recent_findings: Clicked bottom green block (N) at (58,19), then clicked gray dot at (29,22) - green dot appeared in first slot, bottom green block is gone.
open_questions: What order to fill remaining slots? Top order is b, N, Y, p - slots need: blue, yellow, purple.
plan: Fill remaining 3 slots with remaining bottom blocks.
cross_level_notes: None.
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
